Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants to analyze a provided PostgreSQL event log order_event_log orders resources to identify process anomalies hypothesize their root causes and provide SQL queries to investigate these hypotheses further The output should be tailored for an LLM prompt acting as the target LLM doing the analysis

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data and Identify Anomalies Assumed Normal Flow Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment Case 1001 Follows the normal flow perfectly Case 1002 Flow Register Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Issue Invoice Receive Payment Anomalies Confirm Shipment happens before Credit Check and Validate Stock Validate Stock happens after Ship Goods Info attempted early Y in Confirm Shipment Case 1003 Flow Register Perform Credit Check Ship Goods Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Receive Payment Anomalies Ship Goods happens before Confirm Shipment and Validate Stock Validate Stock is missing entirely Info late confirmation Y in Confirm Shipment Case 1004 Flow Register Receive Payment Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Anomalies Receive Payment and Issue Invoice happen before anything else credit stock logistics Validate Stock is missing Info shipment scheduled N in Confirm Shipment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Formulate Hypotheses Hypothesis 1 Bypassing Credit and Stock Checks for specific order types or customers Case 1002 is a priority order Maybe priority orders skip checks or people try to fast track them attempted early Y Hypothesis 2 System integration errors or out of order logging Case 1003 shipped before confirmation late confirmation Y Maybe the warehouse system triggered an event before the logistics manager approved it or there is a lag in event logging Hypothesis 3 Pre payments Exception handling Cash on Delivery or upfront payments Case 1004 received payment and issued invoice immediately Maybe it is a prepaid order but the process still should not ignore stock validation and shipment scheduling Hypothesis 4 Resource role confusion or lack of training Are specific departments such as Logistics triggering events out of sequence Are junior staff making mistakes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Develop SQL Queries to Test Hypotheses Query 1 Identify sequences violating the assumed flow specifically logistics before finance Need to use Window Functions LAG LEAD to find if Credit Check happens after Ship Goods or Confirm Shipment Query 2 Correlate anomalies with Order Types Join order_event_log and orders to see if priority orders have a higher rate of out of sequence events compared to standard ones Query 3 Identify missing activities Check if all cases contain the 7 baseline activities using conditional aggregation COUNT CASE WHEN Specifically looking for missing Validate Stock Query 4 Investigate specific resource behavior Join with resources to see if specific roles departments are responsible for out of sequence timestamps Eg are Logistics Managers confirming shipments too early Query 5 Analyze the Pre payment anomaly pattern Find cases where Receive Payment is the second activity or happens before shipping

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Structure the Output Introduction Acknowledge the task and set the analytical tone Section 1 Identified Anomalies Break down the anomalies case by case or by pattern Out of sequence events Missing steps Highlight specific rows Section 2 Hypotheses Provide logical business system reasons for why these happened Tie them back to the anomalies Section 3 Investigative SQL Queries Present the queries clearly explaining what each query tests and how it works crucial for PostgreSQL context utilizing LAG CTEs conditional aggregation Conclusion Next Steps Brief wrap up

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refine the SQL Queries Self Correction Edge Case Handling during drafting Drafting Query 1 Sequence Violations Finding if Ship Goods happens before Credit Check Better Explicitly look for events where the expected prerequisite timestamp is later than the current event timestamp Drafting Query 3 Missing Activities This is clean and standard Postgres Drafting Query 2 Order Type Correlation Need to flag a case as anomalous then join to orders Lets use a simpler approach for the final output to ensure readability while maintaining power Defining anomaly as Ship Goods before Credit Check or Missing Validate Stock

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Ensure terminology matches the provided schema case_id event_id additional_info Make sure hypotheses sound like a real process mining business analyst eg using terms like fast tracking process drift system decoupling Check PostgreSQL syntax standard SQL works perfectly here but mentioning window functions is good
